product_overview: Grounded is a live demo of a retrieval-augmented generation workflow with claim-by-claim verification and self-correction. It helps users ask questions, inspect groundedness evidence, and compare corrected vs annotated answers while demonstrating how hallucinations are reduced.
core_goals: Ask a question and get a generated answer from the live verification dashboard
Inspect per-claim support verdicts and supporting evidence for an answer
Adjust the support threshold to see how groundedness and answer correction change
Compare corrected and annotated versions of an answer
Try precomputed examples and instant verification flows from the landing page

features.name: Landing page hero verification demo
features.description: The landing page automatically plays a claim-by-claim verification demo that shows supported and unsupported claims, the groundedness meter, and the measured hallucination reduction result.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Observe the hero verification auto-play resolve claims one by one -> See supported claims kept and unsupported claims struck through and dropped -> See the groundedness meter rise and the result change from 34.9% to 13.1%
Navigate to / -> Click replay -> Watch the verification animation run again
Navigate to / -> Use reduced motion settings -> See the final verification state displayed statically

features.name: Verify your own question from the landing page
features.description: Users can enter their own question on the landing page and launch the live dashboard with that question prefilled for verification.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type a question into the verification input -> Submit the question -> Arrive at /app with the question prefilled and see the live verification begin

features.name: Open the live demo dashboard
features.description: Users can move from the landing page to the live verification dashboard using the navigation or example prompts.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select the live demo entry point -> Arrive at /app and see the interactive verification dashboard
Navigate to / -> Choose an example prompt chip -> Arrive at /app with an example loaded for verification

features.name: Precomputed example verification
features.description: The dashboard can load instant precomputed examples that show claim-level verdicts, supporting scores, sources, and the corrected answer without running a live generation pass.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Select an example chip -> See the example load instantly with claim-by-claim support verdicts -> Review support scores, sources, and the corrected answer
Navigate to /app -> Select a different example chip -> See the dashboard update to a new precomputed verification result

features.name: Calibration threshold adjustment
features.description: Users can drag the support threshold on the dashboard to immediately change which claims are treated as supported or unsupported.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Drag the threshold on the calibration rail -> Observe claims flip between supported and unsupported -> See the groundedness percentage update in real time -> See the corrected answer change accordingly
Navigate to /app -> Hover a claim marker -> Read the claim text -> Select a claim marker -> Jump to that claim in the verification view

features.name: Per-claim evidence reveal
features.description: Each verified claim can reveal the most relevant retrieved passage that supports or contradicts it.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Open a claim's evidence section -> See the closest matching passage displayed in a quote box -> Close the evidence section and continue reviewing other claims

features.name: Corrected versus annotated answer toggle
features.description: Users can switch between a cleaned-up corrected answer and an annotated answer that shows unsupported text struck through inline.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Switch to corrected view -> See only grounded content in the answer
Navigate to /app -> Switch to annotated view -> See unsupported claims struck through inline while the rest of the answer remains visible

features.name: Live question asking and verification
features.description: Users can submit a free-form question to run the full retrieve, generate, verify, and correct pipeline, then review the resulting verdicts and answer.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Enter a question in the ask form -> Submit the question -> See the retrieve, generate, and verify loading state -> Review claim-by-claim verdicts and the corrected answer
Navigate to /app -> Enter a question outside the available corpus topics -> Submit the question -> See the system abstain from answering from the provided context

tech_stack: Vanilla JavaScript (no framework, no build step)

tech_stack.CSS3 (design tokens, self-hosted woff2 f: Space Grotesk, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono)

tech_stack: FastAPI (Python backend serving pages + JSON API)
MiniCheck DeBERTa-v3 verifier + Ollama qwen2.5:3b generator + ChromaDB (backend; CPU-only)

routes.description: Landing page. Hero auto-plays a real claim-by-claim verification (the automotive-pay example), catches hallucinated claims, animates a groundedness meter, and ties it to the RAGTruth 34.9% to 13.1% result. Has a "verify your own" input, example chips, how-it-works, score-distribution chart, operating-points table, and CTA.

Live verification dashboard. Ask a question or open a precomputed example, see claim-by-claim supported/unsupported verdicts with support scores, a draggable calibration threshold slider, per-claim evidence reveal, and a corrected/annotated answer toggle. Reads optional ?q= query param to prefill and auto-run.
GET endpoint returning precomputed real-RAGTruth verification examples as JSON (instant, no model inference).

POST endpoint. Body {query}. Runs retrieve to generate to verify to correct and returns answer, corrected answer, per-claim verdicts with support + evidence, groundedness, threshold, sources. Slow (~1 minute, CPU generation).

features.name: Self-playing hero verification (landing)
features.description: On page load the hero readout auto-plays a real verification, resolving claims one at a time to supported (green) or unsupported (red, struck-through, "dropped"), sweeping a scanline, climbing a groundedness meter to 40%, and lighting the 34.9% to 13.1% result. Loops; respects prefers-reduced-motion by showing the final state statically.
user_interactions: Observe the hero readout auto-play and resolve 5 claims (2 kept, 3 caught)
Click the "replay" button to re-run the verification animation
See the groundedness meter and "34.9% to 13.1%" result readout

features.name: Verify your own (landing to dashboard)
features.description: The hero input box submits a question to the dashboard, carrying it as ?q= so the dashboard prefills and auto-runs a verification.
user_interactions: Type a question into the "Verify your own question" input
Click "Verify" to navigate to /app with the query

features.name: Navigate landing to dashboard
features.description: Nav button and example chips link to the live demo dashboard.
user_interactions: Click "Open live demo" in the nav to go to /app
Click an example chip ("automotive pay", "grilling steak", "salary data") to go to /app

features.name: Inspect precomputed example (dashboard)
features.description: Clicking an example chip loads a precomputed real-RAGTruth verification instantly (no model call), rendering claim-by-claim verdicts, the calibration rail, and the corrected answer.
user_interactions: Click an "example N" chip
See claims resolve to supported/unsupported with mono support scores
See sources and note line

features.name: Calibration threshold slider (dashboard signature)
features.description: A draggable brass threshold on a 0-1 support-score axis. Dragging recolors claims supported<->unsupported in real time, updates the groundedness percentage, the drop-zone shading, and the corrected answer.
user_interactions: Drag the threshold range slider on the calibration rail
Observe claims flip supported/unsupported and the percentage update live
Hover a claim tick to see its claim text; click a tick to jump to the claim

features.name: Per-claim evidence reveal (dashboard)
features.description: Each claim has an "evidence" toggle that expands to show the closest passage in the retrieved context that the verifier scored against.
user_interactions: Click the "evidence" toggle under a claim
See the closest supporting/contradicting passage in a quote box

features.name: Corrected vs annotated answer toggle (dashboard)
features.description: A segmented control switches between the clean corrected answer (unsupported claims removed) and the annotated answer (unsupported claims struck through inline).
user_interactions: Click "corrected" or "annotated" in the toggle
See the answer rendering change accordingly

features.name: Ask a live question (dashboard)
features.description: Type a question and submit; the pipeline retrieves, generates, verifies, and corrects, then renders verdicts. Shows a retrieve/generate/verify loading state. Slow on CPU (~1 minute). Out-of-corpus questions abstain.
user_interactions: Type a question in the Ask box and click Ask
See the loading state (retrieve, generate, verify)
See claim-by-claim verdicts and the corrected answer, or an abstention

known_limitations.issue: The /ask endpoint is slow (~1 minute per request) because generation runs on CPU via Ollama, and the server is single-worker.
known_limitations.impact: Live "Ask" tests are slow and should not be run concurrently; example chips and the landing hero are instant alternatives.

known_limitations.issue: No authentication or login of any kind.
known_limitations.impact: All routes are public; there is no login flow to test.

known_limitations.issue: The corpus is only 4 local documents (antikythera, caffeine, coral bleaching, emu war).
known_limitations.impact: Live questions outside those topics will correctly abstain ("I can't answer this from the provided context").
